request.question: The trading comps imply a much higher value than your DCF. Why the gap, and which should the committee trust?

events.text: Lead answer: the gap is largely an artifact of *what each
method is priced off*, not a genuine disagreement about value, and the comparables
pack itself instructs that the DCF is the anchor and comps are only a sense-check. I'
d also flag that neither deliverable actually publishes a comps-implied equity value next to a DCF-implied one
, so I can't quantify the "gap" precisely from the connected sources; what I can do is explain the divergence and tell you

which to weight.

## Why a comps read sits above the DCF

**1. Comps capitalise
today's revenue; the DCF discounts a depressed forward path.** Jahez is in an earnings trough:
Q1-2026 printed a SAR 9.2m net loss, and the balance sheet carries only SAR
16.6m of net cash

events.quote:
Q1-2026 printed a SAR 9.2m net loss. The balance sheet carries
SAR 16.6m of net cash — SAR 510.7m of cash and equivalents, less SAR 161.2m of debt and SAR
332.9m of IFRS-16 lease liabilities.

events.text: , on the back of FY2025 margin compression where
gross margin fell to 22.8% (FY2024A: 24.4%) and adjusted EBITDA margin contracted to 8.3%

events.quote:
Gross margin — the closest disclosed
proxy for contribution margin per order — fell to 22.8% (FY2024A: 24.4%), and adjusted EBITDA margin
contracted to 8.3% (FY2024A: 11.3%).

events.text: . A revenue multiple applied to current sales ignores that trough; a
FCFF model discounts through it. That structurally pulls the DCF below a na

ive multiple read.

**2. The comp set is extraordinarily dispersed, and the high end isn't applicable.**
EV/Revenue ranges from 0.97x (Delivery Hero) to 5.53x (DoorDash), and EV/EBITDA from
12.9x (Talabat) to 60.25x (DoorDash), which largely tracks each platform's profitability profile rather than a single "delivery sector" multiple.

events.quote:
D. Football-Field Note
The comp set shows wide dispersion — EV/Revenue from 0.97x (Delivery Hero, EU-listed, thinner margins) to 5.53x
(DoorDash, US-listed, scale premium), and EV/EBITDA from 12.9x

flect a US scale premium; letting that name pull a comps average upward is what

creates the illusion of a "much higher" value.

**3. The one metric that would normalise across profitability is missing.**
EV/GMV is marked n/a across the board, none of the free sources reliably expose EV alongside a matched-period, matched-currency G
MV figure

events.quote:
C. Trading Comparables
EV/GMV is marked n/a across the board — none of the free sources below reliably expose EV alongside a matched-period,
matched-currency GMV figure; computing it would require e

events.text: , so we can't triangulate on the measure least distorted by the current margin squ

eeze.

**4. The trailing P/E is meaningless right now.**
Trailing P/E of 92.76x is distorted
by depressed trailing-twelve-month earnings, not representative of a normalized multiple.

events.quote: Trailing P/E of 92.76x is distorted by depressed trailing-twelvemonth earnings (see Q1 2026 net loss, Industry & News Pack §3) — not representative of a normalized multiple.

events.text:
## Which the committee should trust

The com
ps pack answers this directly.
Given the incomplete beta/cost-of-deb
t inputs, this comps table should be treated as a sense-check on a primary DCF, not a standalone valuation, flag to IC that neither approach alone resolves a tight range,
and that the WACC build needs the outstanding items closed out before the DCF is final.

events.quote:
Given the incomplete beta/cost-of-debt
inputs in §B, this comps table should be treated as a sense-check on a primary DCF, not a standalone valuation —
flag to IC that neither approach alone resolve

events.text: So: **the DCF is the anchor**, with comps as a band
around it. Within the comps, weight is not equal,
Talabat is the most structurally comparable name (single
-region GCC food-delivery platform, DFM-listed, broadly comparable margin profile) and should carry the most weight of the four; DoorDash and Delivery Hero
are useful range markers but trade on different growth and scale assumptions than a KSA-only platform.

events.quote:
Talabat is the most
structurally comparable name (single-region GCC food-delivery platform, DFM-listed, broadly comparable margin
profile) and should carry the most weight of the four in any comps-b

.88x EV/Revenue, 12.90x EV/EBITDA and 16.70x P/E (TTM)

ober cross-check than the DoorDash end of the range.

## The important caveat, don't over-trust the DCF either
The DCF is built on a WACC whose inputs the comps pack flags as unfinished. The anal
ysis uses
a CAPM-derived WACC of 13.31% (4.6% risk-free + 1.82x comp-
set beta at a 5.0% equity risk premium)

events.quote:
Methodology: a discounted cash flow (FCFF) model, discounted at a CAPM-derived
WACC of 13.31% (4.6% risk-free + 1.82x comp-set beta at a 5.0% equity risk premium, for a 13.7% cost
of equity) with a

events.text: . That 1.82x is effectively the D
oorDash/Delivery Hero average, and the comps pack is explicit that this is not a finished input:
the standard
fix is a bottom-up beta built from unlevered comparable-company betas re-levered to Jahez's capital structure; that requires each comp's debt/
equity and marginal tax rate, which were not cleanly available from free sources in this pass, flagged as a modeling next step rather than estimated here.

events.quote:
The standard fix is a bottom-up beta built
from unlevered comparable-company betas re-levered to Jahez's capital structure; that requires each comp's debt/equity
and marginal tax rate, which were no

events.text: **My judgment (labelled as such):** the "gap" is not ev
idence the stock is deeply undervalued, it's evidence that a current-revenue multiple flatters a company
in an earnings trough while a forward DCF does not. I'd tell the IC to anchor on the DCF,
use a *Talabat-weighted* comps band (not a four-name average, and explicitly excluding the
DoorDash high end) as the sanity check, and treat neither as final until the bottom-up beta and cost-of-debt inputs in the
WACC build are closed. What would change this view: a completed re-levered beta and a sourced cost of debt,
if those move the WACC materially, the DCF value (and therefore the size of any
apparent gap) moves with it.
